accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context about section 546 of the PHSA first responder training program.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the program description and statute identification are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes and modifies the first responder training grant program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly describes amendments to section 546 of the Public Health Service Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the fiscal years 2026 through 2030 authorization period.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all operative effects: language broadening, opioid-reference removals, capitalization change, and reauthorization.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone with no editorializing.

concise.why: No padding or repetition; appropriately compact.

correct_entities.why: Public Health Service Act and section 546 are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Correctly states $36 million for FY2019-2023 replaced by $57 million for FY2026-2030.
